claim: GPT-4.5 was likely trained in late 2023 based on October 2023 knowledge cutoff, making it months old compared to Claude 3.7 (Oct 2024) and other recent models
actionable: When evaluating models, check knowledge cutoff dates—older models may underperform on current tasks despite marketing claims.
source_title: How can GPT-4.5 be So Bad?

claim: GPT-4.5 pricing ($75 per million input tokens, $150 per output) is 30x higher than GPT-4o but benchmarks show inferior performance on SWE-Bench and math tasks
actionable: Evaluate models on total cost-of-ownership including latency and accuracy, not just benchmark position; GPT-4.5 may not justify its price premium.
source_title: How can GPT-4.5 be So Bad?

claim: Reasoning models (o3-mini, Claude 3.7 Sonnet, DeepSeekR1) outperform standard scale-up models like GPT-4.5 on complex benchmarks despite smaller base size
actionable: For complex reasoning tasks, prefer reasoning-enabled models over large standard models; test-time compute scaling beats pure parameter scaling.
source_title: How can GPT-4.5 be So Bad?

claim: GPT-4.5 latency is slow (1+ minute on test) compared to GPT-4o (13 seconds) and GPT-4o-mini (6.5 seconds), making it impractical for interactive use
actionable: Benchmark inference latency alongside accuracy before adopting new models; slow inference kills user experience even with better outputs.
source_title: How can GPT-4.5 be So Bad?
